=== THE ANTI-OVERFIT LAW (Sol V11) ===
The validator decides ADMIT by hardcoded WORD LISTS, and every unlisted word is a HOLE. Proven live
(judge forced UNCERTAIN), all ADMITTED today:
  'The Cambridge cohort uncovered that the treatment cured disease.'
  'The treatment eradicated disease.'   heading 'Daily aspirin eradicates disease'
  synthesis 'These studies observe different units, and the intervention eradicates disease.'
THE GENERAL RULE: a LIST MAY ONLY REJECT (cheap fail-safe). ABSENCE OF A LIST HIT MEANS UNKNOWN -> route
to the judge, NEVER admit. ADMISSION REQUIRES POSITIVE PROOF (structural, or a semantic judge verdict with
governing context, fail-closed on UNCERTAIN/unavailable). Even a BYTE-IDENTICAL quotation may not be
UPGRADED into a finding -- the speech act (a hypothesis, a question, someone else's claim) is dropped.
Never weaken a test to pass. `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` and test_fabrication_paths.py stay
the source of truth; a green that hides a false admit is the worst outcome.


YOU OWN /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py AND /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cohesion_pass.py (separate files, no other
agent touches them).

ARGUMENT_PLANNER (Sol V11): these secondhand/forecast spans are fully eligible today and must NOT be:
   'Brown demonstrated that the drug reduced mortality.' (secondhand -- Brown reporting someone else)
   'The drug is anticipated to reduce mortality.' / 'is poised to improve survival.' (forecast, not result)
FIX: contract vocabularies (outcome/polarity/negator/clause-break, secondhand_cues:256, forecast_cues)
NOMINATE CANDIDATE FACETS ONLY. A semantic facet extractor returns {value, exact supporting span,
confidence/verdict}; only AFFIRMATIVE, UNAMBIGUOUS bindings enter comparisons. Secondhand-ownership and
observed-vs-forecast status must be JUDGED for every candidate span (unlisted 'was anticipated to lower
mortality' -> forecast/unknown, not comparable). Digit presence is NOT an estimate; a sample size / trial
number cannot make a span a quantitative effect. Corpus absence -> 'not represented in this corpus' only.
Note: the default contract (:141, also loaded by report_ast) is task-72-specific -- flag it; a configurable
default does not make the path general.

COHESION_PASS (Sol V11): templates CREATE new factual relations. These PASS today and must not:
   'their agreement carries more weight than either alone.' / '...the answer is not the same one.' /
   'over the long-term horizon the same mechanisms need not hold.'
FIX: a premise-free cohesion node may express ONLY document structure ('The next section considers safety
outcomes.'). ANY statement about evidence/comparability/agreement/strength/limits needs premise IDs + a
relation proof. Generate transitions from PROVED conclusions, not dominant metadata. DISABLE paragraph
reordering unless cross-paragraph anaphora + discourse dependencies are represented and preserved -- safe
default is NO reorder (reorder can change the antecedent of 'this result'/'the former').
VERIFY: the secondhand/forecast planner spans -> not eligible/judged; the 3 cohesion sentences REJECT; a
structural transition ('The next section considers...') SHIPS; reorder is off by default. Canary green, no
test weakened. Do NOT touch report_ast/synthesis_contract. Do NOT commit. Report file:line + real output.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE ARGUMENT PLANNER — decides WHAT IS BEING COMPARED WITH WHAT, before a word of prose exists.
3	
4	THE DEFECT THIS FIXES
5	---------------------
6	`cellcog_composer.write_report` fans 28 subsections out to 6 threads and generates every one of them
7	INDEPENDENTLY. There is no shared argument state. Nobody, anywhere in the pipeline, ever decides what
8	is being compared with what. So the report LISTS findings instead of ADJUDICATING them, and Critical
9	Synthesis came out at 210 words of 8,012 -- 2.6% of the report for 8% of the score -- and scored 6.36
10	on the joint-heaviest criterion (w=0.0800).
11	
12	A later cohesion pass CANNOT repair this. It cannot manufacture a comparison the planner never made.
13	So the comparison is selected HERE, from the cards, BEFORE the writer is called.
14	
15	THE TRAP, AND WHY MOST OF THIS FILE IS REFUSALS
16	-----------------------------------------------
17	The seductive failure is the FALSE RECONCILIATION:
18	
19	    "These findings only look contradictory -- they measure different units."
20	
21	...asserted about two cards that were never in conflict, or whose 'units' were guessed. That sentence
22	is a fabrication with no fabricated particular in it: every name is real, every number is real, and the
23	RELATION is invented. It is the exact shape of the bug that already shipped once here (a real mechanism,
24	'task displacement', bound to a real paper, Bresnahan 2002, that never states it). THE LIE IS IN THE
25	BINDING. A planner is a machine for producing bindings, so a planner is a machine for producing that lie.
26	
27	Three rules hold it shut, and they are enforced in code, not in the prompt:
28	
29	  1. THE SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. Every facet is derived from the card's VERBATIM SPAN or from the
30	     card's DECLARED metadata. `claim` is model-authored ("state the finding in your words") and is
31	     NEVER read on any derivation path -- `derive_facets()` takes a `span: str`, so it is structurally
32	     incapable of seeing the claim. `has_number` is likewise claim-derived and is NOT TRUSTED: this
33	     module recomputes quantitativeness from the span. (Measured: 29 spans carry a span-verified figure;
34	     only 16 cards are flagged. The flag reads the paraphrase, and the paraphrase dropped the number.)
35	
36	  2. A MISSING TAG IS NOT A COMPARISON. If the axis a bundle turns on is not present on both cards, the
37	     bundle is not emitted as a comparison -- it is emitted as NOT_A_COMPARISON with the reason. We do
38	     not infer a level, a horizon or a method in order to have something to say. Measured on this corpus:
39	     43% of cards declare NO horizon, and geography is present in 3 spans out of 133 -- so geography can
40	     key NOTHING here, and the planner says so instead of inventing it.
41	
42	  3. YOU MAY ONLY RECONCILE AN APPARENT CONFLICT. "They only look contradictory" presupposes that they
43	     LOOK contradictory. That requires both directions to be KNOWN and OPPOSITE. Direction is derived
44	     from the span by a windowed, negation-guarded polarity rule that returns UNKNOWN on any ambiguity
45	     (measured: 82/133 spans carry no polarity cue at all and 15 fire both, so ~74% of cards are
46	     direction-UNKNOWN and can never enter a conflict). Where the directions are not both known, the
47	     bundle is still a real comparison -- but the planner emits the NON-reconciling verdict, which says
48	     the estimates bear on different units and does NOT claim they agree.
49	
50	WHAT IT EMITS
51	-------------
52	A SENTENCE IR the writer must fill, so VOICE IS CARRIED STRUCTURALLY instead of being guessed back out
53	of the prose by a regex downstream:
54	
55	    {voice: "attributed"|"owned", text, source_clauses:[{card_id, clause_text}], premise_card_ids:[]}
56	
57	  * ATTRIBUTED sentences bind to a CARD_ID, never to a surname. Surname binding is ambiguous and this
58	    corpus proves it: "Acemoglu" names BOTH the 2018 AER robots paper and the 2019 JEP paper, and
59	    `_cited_cards` returns whichever happens to come first in the list. A figure from one can be gated
60	    against the span of the other. Five surnames here are bound to two papers each.
61	  * OWNED sentences carry `source_clauses: []` and are checked to name NO author in the entire corpus.
62	    An OWNED verdict may never silently inherit a source attribution -- that is how the reviewer's own
63	    voice gets laundered into a citation.
64	  * Every OWNED verdict is run through `synthesis_contract.validate()` HERE, at plan time. The planner
65	    is therefore incapable of planning a sentence the composer's gate would delete -- which is the
66	    reason the synthesis section starved in the first place. What survives is a guaranteed floor.
67	
68	GENERAL, NOT TASK-72
69	--------------------
70	Nothing about this question is compiled into the logic. The facet vocabularies, the polarity cues, the
71	design ranking and the outline live in a RESEARCH CONTRACT (`ResearchContract`) -- data, compiled from
72	the question, loadable with --contract. The engine reads the contract. Swap the contract, plan any
73	question.
74	
75	    python scripts/argument_planner.py                 # plan over outputs/evidence_cards.json
76	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --bundles       # just the comparison bundles it found
77	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --plans         # the per-subsection plans
78	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --ir SUB        # the sentence IR for one subsection
79	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --self-test     # the adversarial suite (false reconciliations)
80	"""
81	from __future__ import annotations
82	
83	import argparse
84	import itertools
85	import json
86	import re
87	import sys
88	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
89	from pathlib import Path
90	
91	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
92	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
93	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
94	
95	from synthesis_contract import (Premise, Synthesis, validate, OPERATIONS,  # noqa: E402
96	                                RelationProof, classify_claim, prove, level_span_support)
97	
98	CARDS_V2 = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_v2.json'
99	CARDS_V1 = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards.json'
100	
101	
102	def cards_path() -> Path:
103	    return CARDS_V2 if CARDS_V2.exists() else CARDS_V1
104	
105	
106	# ===================================================================== THE RESEARCH CONTRACT
107	#
108	# DATA, NOT LOGIC. Compiled from the question; the engine below never mentions this question.
109	# A facet is either DECLARED (it is a field the card already carries, gated at extraction) or
110	# SPAN-DERIVED (a surface form matched VERBATIM in the span, recorded so a human can audit it).
111	# There is no third kind. In particular there is no INFERRED facet, because an inferred facet is
112	# exactly what manufactures a false reconciliation.
113	
114	@dataclass
115	class ResearchContract:
116	    question: str
117	    declared_facets: dict[str, str]                       # facet -> card field it reads
118	    span_facets: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]]          # facet -> value -> [regex]
119	    polarity: dict[str, list[str]]                        # direction class -> [regex]
120	    negators: list[str]
121	    design_rank: dict[str, int]                           # method -> evidential strength
122	    empirical_designs: list[str]                          # designs that produce an ESTIMATE
123	    secondhand_cues: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # the span reports ANOTHER paper's finding
124	    forecast_cues: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # the span is a projection, not a finding
125	    # ADJUDICATIVE SUBSECTIONS are about the ARGUMENT, not about a topic. "Where the literature
126	    # genuinely disagrees" shares no content word with any card, so a lexical matcher hands it NOTHING --
127	    # and that subsection is the one this whole planner exists to feed (Critical Synthesis: 210 words of
128	    # 8,012, scoring 6.36 on the joint-heaviest criterion). These are matched on the vocabulary of
129	    # ADJUDICATION, which is general to any research question, and they get FIRST PICK of the bundles.
130	    adjudicative_roles: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
131	    outcome_facet: str = 'outcome'                        # which facet is the dependent variable
132	    outline: list[tuple[str, list[str]]] = field(default_factory=list)
133	
134	    @staticmethod
135	    def load(p: Path) -> 'ResearchContract':
136	        d = json.loads(p.read_text())
137	        d['outline'] = [(s, subs) for s, subs in d.get('outline', [])]
138	        return ResearchContract(**d)
139	
140	
141	def default_contract() -> ResearchContract:
142	    """Compiled from: 'the restructuring impact of AI on the labor market'.
143	
144	    Every vocabulary below is a list of surface forms we expect to find IN A SPAN. They are matched
145	    against the verbatim span and the matched form is recorded. They are NOT a taxonomy of the world;
146	    they are a taxonomy of what the papers actually SAY, which is the only thing we are allowed to key on.
147	    """
148	    try:
149	        from cellcog_composer import OUTLINE            # the outline the composer will actually write
150	    except Exception:
151	        OUTLINE = []
152	    return ResearchContract(
153	        question='the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence on the labor market',
154	        # ---- DECLARED: gated at extraction, read straight off the card. NEVER inferred here.
155	        declared_facets={'unit_of_analysis': 'level', 'method': 'method', 'horizon': 'horizon'},
156	        # ---- SPAN-DERIVED: matched verbatim in the span; the matched form is kept as evidence.
157	        span_facets={
158	            'technology': {
159	                'robotics':        [r'\brobot\w*'],
160	                'generative_ai':   [r'generative ai\w*', r'large language model\w*', r'\bllms?\b',
161	                                    r'chatgpt', r'\bgpt-?\d?\b'],
162	                'ai_ml':           [r'artificial intelligence', r'\bai\b', r'machine learning',
163	                                    r'deep learning', r'prediction machine\w*', r'\balgorithm\w*'],
164	                'ict_computing':   [r'computer\w*', r'information technolog\w*', r'\bict\b',
165	                                    r'software', r'digiti[sz]\w*', r'computeri[sz]\w*'],
166	                'automation':      [r'automat\w*'],
167	            },
168	            'outcome': {
169	                # `\bjobs?\b` matched "JOB SATISFACTION" and `\bemploy\w*` matched "EMPLOYEE EXPERIENCE".
170	                # Neither is the quantity of employment, and both were feeding a fabricated conflict.
171	                # An outcome pattern must name the QUANTITY, not merely share a word with it.
172	                'employment':      [r'\bemployment\b', r'\bunemploy\w*', r'\bjob loss\w*',
173	                                    r'\bjobs?\b(?!\s+(?:satisfaction|security|quality|content|title|'
174	                                    r'description|design))',
175	                                    r'\bworkforce\b', r'\bhiring\b', r'\blab(?:o|ou)r demand\b'],
176	                'wages':           [r'wages?\b', r'earnings?\b', r'salar\w*', r'\bincome\b',
177	                                    r'compensation'],
178	                'productivity':    [r'productivity', r'output per', r'efficiency'],
179	                'skills':          [r'skills?\b', r'skilled\b', r'reskill\w*', r'upskill\w*',
180	                                    r'human capital', r'competenc\w*', r'training'],
181	                'tasks':           [r'tasks?\b', r'routine\b', r'job content'],
182	                'labor_share':     [r'lab(?:o|ou)r share'],
183	                'inequality':      [r'inequalit\w*', r'polari[sz]\w*', r'disparit\w*', r'wage gap'],
184	            },
185	            'industry': {
186	                'manufacturing':   [r'manufactur\w*', r'\bfactor(?:y|ies)\b', r'assembly line',
187	                                    r'industrial robot\w*', r'\bplants?\b'],
188	                'healthcare':      [r'health ?care', r'\bmedical\b', r'\bclinical\b', r'\bnurs\w*',
189	                                    r'\bpatients?\b', r'physician\w*'],
190	                'finance':         [r'\bfinanc\w*', r'\bbank\w*', r'insurance', r'fintech'],
191	                'retail':          [r'\bretail\w*', r'e-commerce', r'\bstores?\b'],
192	                'education':       [r'education\w*', r'\bschools?\b', r'\bteach\w*', r'\bstudents?\b'],
193	                'transport':       [r'transport\w*', r'\bdrivers?\b', r'logistics?\b', r'\btrucks?\b',
194	                                    r'autonomous vehicle\w*'],
195	                'creative':        [r'\bcreative\b', r'journalis\w*', r'\bdesigners?\b', r'\bartists?\b'],
196	                'prof_services':   [r'professional service\w*', r'\bconsult\w*', r'\blegal\b', r'\blawyer\w*'],
197	                'agriculture':     [r'agricultur\w*', r'\bfarm\w*'],
198	            },
199	            'geography': {
200	                'united_states':   [r'united states', r'\bu\.s\.', r'\bus\b', r'american\b'],
201	                'europe':          [r'\beurope\w*', r'\bgermany\b', r'\bfrance\b', r'united kingdom',
202	                                    r'\bnordic\b', r'\beu\b'],
203	                'china':           [r'\bchina\b', r'\bchinese\b'],
204	                'developing':      [r'developing countr\w*', r'\bemerging econom\w*', r'\bglobal south\b'],
205	            },
206	        },
207	        # ---- DIRECTION: the most dangerous facet on the board. See derive_direction().
208	        #
209	        #      A POLARITY CUE MUST PREDICATE A CHANGE OF THE OUTCOME. It may not merely be a word with
210	        #      a direction-ish flavour sitting near it. The first build shipped `\bincreas\w*`, which
211	        #      matched the ADVERB in "employment of the less-skilled is increasingly DEPENDENT on
212	        #      physical proximity" -- where 'increasingly' modifies 'dependent' and says nothing whatever
213	        #      about the level of employment -- and `\bhigher\b`/`\blower\w*`, which match the COMPARATIVE
214	        #      ADJECTIVE in "higher-skilled workers". Those two cues, between them, produced this
215	        #      planner's top-ranked GENUINE CONFLICT out of two cards that were not in tension and were
216	        #      not even about the same thing.
217	        #      So: verbs and magnitude nouns only. No adverbs, no comparative adjectives.
218	        polarity={
219	            'negative': [r'\breduc(?:e|es|ed|ing|tion|tions)\b', r'\bdeclin(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',
220	                         r'\bfell\b', r'\bfalls?\b', r'\bfalling\b', r'\bdecreas(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',
221	                         r'\bdisplac(?:e|es|ed|ing|ement)\b', r'\bloss(?:es)?\b', r'\bshrink\w*',
222	                         r'\bshrank\b', r'\bsubstitut(?:e|es|ed|ing|ion)\b', r'\bdestroy\w*',
223	                         r'\berod(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b', r'\bnegative\b', r'\bredundant\b', r'\bobsolete\b',
224	                         r'\breplac(?:e|es|ed|ing|ement)\b', r'\beliminat(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b'],
225	            'positive': [r'\bincreas(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',          # NOT "increasingly"
226	                         r'\brais(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b', r'\bris(?:e|es|ing)\b', r'\brose\b',
227	                         r'\bgrow(?:s|ing|th)?\b', r'\bgrew\b', r'\bgains?\b',
228	                         r'\bcreat(?:e|es|ed|ing|ion)\b', r'\bcomplement(?:s|ed|ing|arity)?\b',
229	                         r'\bexpand(?:s|ed|ing)?\b', r'\bexpansion\b', r'\bimprov(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',
230	                         r'\baugment(?:s|ed|ing)?\b', r'\bpositive\b'],
231	            'null':     [r'\bno significant\b', r'\bno effect\b', r'\bno evidence\b', r'\binsignificant\b',
232	                         r'\btoo small to detect\b', r'\bnot significant\b', r'\bunchanged\b'],
233	        },
234	        # ---- NEGATORS *AND ATTENUATORS*. An attenuator inverts a cue as surely as a negator does:
235	        #      "the SLOWER GROWTH of employment ... accounted for by an acceleration in the DISPLACEMENT
236	        #      effect" (Acemoglu and Restrepo 2019) is a finding about employment being DESTROYED, and the
237	        #      bare cue 'growth' read it as POSITIVE. We do not flip on these -- flipping is inference --
238	        #      we DISCARD the cue and return UNKNOWN, which is the one answer that cannot be wrong.
239	        negators=[r'\bnot\b', r'\bno\b', r'\bnor\b', r'\bnever\b', r'\bwithout\b', r'\bneither\b',
240	                  r'\bfails? to\b', r'\bunable to\b', r'\bcannot\b', r"\bdoes ?n[o']t\b", r'\blittle\b',
241	                  r'\bslow(?:er|ing|ly)?\b', r'\bweak(?:er|ening)?\b', r'\bless\b', r'\bslower\b',
242	                  r'\bdampen\w*', r'\bmuted\b', r'\bmodest\b', r'\blimited\b'],
243	        # ---- an ESTIMATE from a stronger design outranks one from a weaker design. DECLARED fields only.
244	        design_rank={'experiment': 5, 'quasi-experimental': 4, 'observational': 3, 'survey': 2,
245	                     'review': 1, 'theory': 0},
246	        empirical_designs=['experiment', 'quasi-experimental', 'observational', 'survey'],
247	        # ---- THE SECOND-HAND SPAN. A span can be VERBATIM IN THE PAPER and still not be the paper's
248	        #      own finding: a literature-review sentence inside Damioli 2021 reads "Graetz and Michaels
249	        #      (2018) use country-level data ... and find that ...". The extraction gate passes it --
250	        #      the text IS in the paper -- and the composer then prints "Writing in the Eurasian Business
251	        #      Review in 2021, Damioli et al. show that ..." over GRAETZ AND MICHAELS' FINDING AND THEIR
252	        #      FIGURES. That is a fabricated binding of exactly the kind this codebase already bled for
253	        #      ('task displacement' credited to Bresnahan), and no span check can see it, because the
254	        #      span check asks "is this text in the paper?" and never "is this finding the paper's OWN?"
255	        #      MEASURED: 5 such cards, 6 figures that would print under the wrong paper's name.
256	        secondhand_cues=[
257	            r'\b[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+(?:and|&)\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)?\s*\(\s*(?:19|20)\d\d\s*\)',
258	            r'\bet al\.\s*\(\s*(?:19|20)\d\d', r'\bcommission\s*\(\s*(?:19|20)\d\d',
259	            r'\btheir (?:results|findings|study|analysis|paper|estimates|data)\b',
260	            r'\baccording to\b', r'\bhas predicted\b', r'\bhave (?:shown|found|argued|documented)\b',
261	            r'\b(?:studies|scholars|researchers|authors|others) (?:have )?(?:show|find|argue|suggest)',
262	            r'\b(?:world economic forum|mckinsey|oecd|gartner|pwc|deloitte)\b',
263	        ],
264	        # ---- A PROJECTION IS NOT A FINDING. synthesis_contract already treats a forecast as fabrication
265	        #      when the REVIEWER writes one; it is no more ADJUDICABLE as evidence. "AI will make 75
266	        #      million jobs redundant" cannot be weighed against a measured estimate -- there is nothing
267	        #      to weigh, because nothing was measured. But a forecast is still CITABLE ("Agrawal and
268	        #      colleagues argue that AI will affect labor"), so this is the SOFTER penalty: barred from
269	        #      comparisons, admitted as attributed prose. Barring it outright starves the corpus, which
270	        #      is the failure this codebase already suffered when a gate ate its own evidence.
271	        #
272	        #      NOTE THE PATTERNS. A first draft used bare `\bpredict\w*` and it deleted Agrawal, Gans and
273	        #      Goldfarb entirely -- because "a PREDICTION technology" and "the cost of PREDICTION" are the
274	        #      SUBJECT MATTER of Prediction Machines, not forecasts. The noun sense is the whole framework
275	        #      and an outline subsection is named after it. Only the ATTRIBUTIVE, forward-looking forms
276	        #      count; `project` and `prediction` as nouns are technical vocabulary and are left alone.
277	        forecast_cues=[r'\bwill\b', r'\bis going to\b', r'\bby 20[3-9]\d\b',
278	                       r'\b(?:is|are|was|were|has been|have been) (?:predicted|expected|projected|'
279	                       r'forecast) to\b', r'\b(?:predicts|predicted|forecasts|projects) that\b',
280	                       r'\bhas predicted\b', r'\bexpected to\b'],
281	        # ---- WHICH SUBSECTIONS ADJUDICATE, AND ON WHAT. Keyed on the vocabulary of ARGUMENT (disagree,
282	        #      establish, resolve, gap), which belongs to no particular research question.
283	        adjudicative_roles={
284	            r'genuinely disagree|disagree|conflict|contested|tension|contradict':
285	                ['SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION', 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT'],
286	            r'establish|converge|robust|consensus|what the evidence (?:shows|establishes)':
287	                ['SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD', 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON',
288	                 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT'],
289	            r'cannot yet resolve|unresolved|cannot resolve|does not settle|gap|agenda|research agenda':
290	                ['UNCOUNTERED', 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON'],
291	            r'diverge|why .* differ|reconcile':
292	                ['SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT', 'SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION'],
293	        },
294	        outline=OUTLINE,
295	    )

300	@dataclass(frozen=True)
301	class Facet:
302	    name: str
303	    value: str = ''
304	    provenance: str = 'missing'      # 'declared' | 'span' | 'missing'
305	    evidence: str = ''               # the VERBATIM surface form lifted from the span
306	
307	    @property
308	    def known(self) -> bool:
309	        return self.provenance != 'missing' and bool(self.value)
310	
311	    def __str__(self) -> str:
312	        if not self.known:
313	            return f'{self.name}=MISSING'
314	        tag = 'declared' if self.provenance == 'declared' else f'span:"{self.evidence}"'
315	        return f'{self.name}={self.value} ({tag})'
316	
317	
318	DIR_WINDOW = 8      # a polarity cue must sit within this many tokens of an outcome cue
319	NEG_WINDOW = 3      # a negator this close BEFORE a cue voids the cue (we discard; we never flip)
320	
321	# A POLARITY FLIPS AT A CONTRASTIVE CONNECTIVE. "Robots reduce employment while raising productivity"
322	# is one span carrying two opposite findings about two different quantities, and a cue may only be read
323	# as the direction of an outcome that stands IN ITS OWN CLAUSE. This is the same reading
324	# `cellcog_composer._gate_multi` already takes of a comparative sentence -- a conjunction of clauses,
325	# each answerable for itself.
326	_CLAUSE_BREAK = re.compile(
327	    r'[;:]|\bwhile\b|\bwhereas\b|\bbut\b|\balthough\b|\bthough\b|\bhowever\b|\byet\b|'
328	    r'\bby contrast\b|\bin contrast\b|\bon the other hand\b|\beven as\b|\bwhile\b', re.I)
329	
330	
331	def _tokens(s: str) -> list[str]:
332	    return re.findall(r"[a-z0-9''.\-]+", s.lower())
333	
334	
335	def _clauses(span: str) -> list[str]:
336	    return [c for c in _CLAUSE_BREAK.split(span) if c and c.strip()]
337	
338	
339	def _match_spans(text: str, patterns: list[str]) -> list[tuple[int, str]]:
340	    """Return (token_index, matched_surface_form) for every pattern hit. Operates on the SPAN."""
341	    out = []
342	    low = text.lower()
343	    toks = _tokens(low)
344	    # map char offset -> token index
345	    idx, pos = [], 0
346	    for t in toks:
347	        p = low.find(t, pos)
348	        if p < 0:
349	            p = pos
350	        idx.append(p)
351	        pos = p + len(t)
352	    for pat in patterns:
353	        for m in re.finditer(pat, low):
354	            ti = 0
355	            for i, ch in enumerate(idx):
356	                if ch <= m.start():
357	                    ti = i
358	                else:
359	                    break
360	            out.append((ti, m.group(0)))
361	    return out
362	
363	
364	def derive_span_facet(span: str, name: str, vocab: dict[str, list[str]]) -> Facet:
365	    """Match a facet in the VERBATIM SPAN. Records the surface form so a human can audit the tag.
366	
367	    NOTE THE SIGNATURE: it takes a `span: str`. It cannot see `claim`, so it cannot launder the
368	    model's paraphrase into evidence. That is the point.
369	    """
370	    hits: dict[str, tuple[int, str]] = {}
371	    for value, pats in vocab.items():
372	        ms = _match_spans(span, pats)
373	        if ms:
374	            hits[value] = min(ms, key=lambda x: x[0])
375	    if not hits:
376	        return Facet(name)
377	    if len(hits) > 1:
378	        # Several values fire. Take the EARLIEST-mentioned as primary but record the ambiguity: an
379	        # ambiguous facet is still usable as a SHARED facet, and is refused as a comparison AXIS.
380	        best = min(hits.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1][0])
381	        return Facet(name, best[0], 'span', best[1][1])
382	    v, (_, form) = next(iter(hits.items()))
383	    return Facet(name, v, 'span', form)
384	
385	
386	def derive_span_facet_all(span: str, name: str, vocab: dict[str, list[str]]) -> list[str]:
387	    """EVERY value of this facet the span mentions (a span can be about two industries)."""
388	    return sorted(v for v, pats in vocab.items() if _match_spans(span, pats))
389

391	def derive_outcome_direction(span: str, contract: ResearchContract) -> tuple[Facet, Facet, list[str]]:
392	    """OUTCOME AND DIRECTION ARE ONE JOINT DERIVATION, OR THEY ARE NOTHING.
393	
394	    Derived separately, they need not even be about each other. That is not a hypothetical: this exact
395	    span, from Acemoglu and Restrepo 2018 --
396	
397	        "In an extension with heterogeneous SKILLS, we show that INEQUALITY INCREASES during
398	         transitions driven by automation"
399	
400	    was tagged outcome=SKILLS (the earliest outcome word in the span) and direction=POSITIVE (from
401	    'increases', which belongs to INEQUALITY). Neither tag was about the other, and the pair went on to
402	    become the planner's top-ranked GENUINE CONFLICT. Earliest-mention-wins is an arbitrary rule, and an
403	    arbitrary rule applied to the most dangerous facet on the board produces exactly what you would expect.
404	
405	    THE OUTCOME OF A FINDING IS THE QUANTITY WHOSE CHANGE IS REPORTED. So we look for (outcome, polarity)
406	    pairs that stand together in one clause, and let the pair decide both facets at once:
407	
408	      * exactly one outcome, exactly one polarity   -> A DIRECTIONAL FINDING. Both facets assigned.
409	        (Acemoglu above now resolves correctly to outcome=inequality, direction=positive.)
410	      * one outcome, MIXED polarity                 -> outcome assigned, direction UNKNOWN.
411	      * SEVERAL outcomes, each with a direction     -> the span reports several findings and we cannot
412	        say which is THE finding. AMBIGUOUS: citable, never a comparison term.
413	      * no polarity anywhere                        -> a TOPICAL mention, not a finding. Direction UNKNOWN.
414	        The outcome is assigned only if the span mentions exactly ONE outcome family; if it mentions
415	        several with nothing to choose between them, it is AMBIGUOUS and cannot anchor a bundle.
416	
417	    MEASURED ON THIS CORPUS: 17 cards of 133 are directional findings. 15 report several directed
418	    outcomes at once. 98 report no direction at all. The scarcity is the point -- it is what makes a
419	    "genuine conflict" a claim that has to be earned.
420	    """
421	    vocab = contract.span_facets.get('outcome', {})
422	    if not vocab:
423	        return Facet('outcome'), Facet('direction'), []
424	
425	    # A POLARITY CUE GOVERNS EXACTLY ONE QUANTITY: THE NEAREST ONE. Attaching it to every outcome inside
426	    # the window is what bound 'increases' to SKILLS at a distance of seven tokens, when it plainly
427	    # belongs to INEQUALITY at a distance of one. Nearest-cue attachment is the whole fix.
428	    pairs: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}       # outcome -> {polarity: matched_form}
429	    first_seen: dict[str, int] = {}
430	    for clause in _clauses(span):
431	        neg_idx = {i for i, _ in _match_spans(clause, contract.negators)}
432	        occ: list[tuple[int, str]] = []                            # (token_index, outcome_value)
433	        for ov, pats in vocab.items():
434	            for i, _ in _match_spans(clause, pats):
435	                occ.append((i, ov))
436	        if not occ:
437	            continue
438	        for i, ov in occ:
439	            first_seen.setdefault(ov, i)
440	            first_seen[ov] = min(first_seen[ov], i)
441	        for cls, ppats in contract.polarity.items():
442	            for ti, form in _match_spans(clause, ppats):
443	                if any(0 < ti - ni <= NEG_WINDOW for ni in neg_idx):
444	                    continue                                       # negated: DISCARD, never flip
445	                oi, ov = min(occ, key=lambda x: abs(ti - x[0]))    # the NEAREST outcome, and only it
446	                if abs(ti - oi) > DIR_WINDOW:
447	                    continue                                       # too far to govern anything here
448	                pairs.setdefault(ov, {}).setdefault(cls, form)
449	
450	    mentioned = derive_span_facet_all(span, 'outcome', vocab)
451	    if not mentioned:
452	        return Facet('outcome'), Facet('direction'), []
453	
454	    # THE PRIMARY OUTCOME IS THE ONE WHOSE CHANGE IS REPORTED -- the earliest outcome that actually
455	    # carries a direction. Only when nothing carries a direction do we fall back to the earliest
456	    # mention, and then the card is TOPICAL: direction UNKNOWN, so it can never enter a conflict.
457	    directed = [ov for ov in sorted(pairs, key=lambda v: first_seen.get(v, 10 ** 6))]
458	    ov = directed[0] if directed else min(mentioned, key=lambda v: first_seen.get(v, 10 ** 6))
459	    o_form = derive_span_facet(span, 'outcome', {ov: vocab[ov]}).evidence
460	    o_facet = Facet('outcome', ov, 'span', o_form)
461	
462	    pol = pairs.get(ov, {})
463	    if len(pol) != 1:
464	        return o_facet, Facet('direction'), []     # no direction, or this outcome moves both ways
465	    cls, form = next(iter(pol.items()))
466	    return o_facet, Facet('direction', cls, 'span', form), []
467

469	def derive_direction(span: str, contract: ResearchContract, outcome_vocab: list[str]) -> Facet:
470	    """THE FACET THAT MANUFACTURES FALSE CONFLICTS. It returns UNKNOWN at the first sign of trouble.
471	
472	    A DIRECTION IS ALWAYS THE DIRECTION *OF AN OUTCOME*. A bare "positive" is not a fact about the
473	    world; it is a fact about some quantity, and if we do not know which quantity, we know nothing.
474	    So `outcome_vocab` is the vocabulary of THIS CARD'S PRIMARY OUTCOME -- not of outcomes in general.
475	    The first version of this function took every outcome pattern at once, and duly read
476	
477	        "the model improves prediction accuracy; employment is measured separately"
478	
479	    as a POSITIVE finding about EMPLOYMENT, because 'improves' fell within eight tokens of 'employment'.
480	    Pair that card with any negative employment card and the planner would have "discovered" a conflict
481	    between a paper about prediction accuracy and a paper about jobs, then dissolved it by pointing at
482	    the unit of analysis. A false reconciliation, assembled entirely from true particulars, and no
483	    downstream gate on earth would catch it: every word of the resulting sentence is true.
484	
485	    A direction is assigned ONLY when, in the verbatim span:
486	       (a) a polarity cue and a cue for THIS CARD'S OUTCOME stand IN THE SAME CLAUSE (polarity flips at
487	           a contrastive connective, so a cue may not reach across one) and within DIR_WINDOW tokens;
488	       (b) no negator sits within NEG_WINDOW tokens before the cue -- a negated cue is DISCARDED, never
489	           FLIPPED, because flipping is an inference and inference is what we are refusing;
490	       (c) exactly ONE polarity class survives across all such clauses. A span that reports the outcome
491	           falling in one clause and rising in another is UNKNOWN, not a conflict we get to adjudicate.
492	    Measured on this corpus: 82/133 spans carry no polarity cue at all. Roughly three quarters of the
493	    corpus is direction-UNKNOWN and can never enter a conflict bundle. That is the honest number, and it
494	    is the whole reason this function exists.
495	    """
496	    if not outcome_vocab:
497	        return Facet('direction')
498	    neg_pats = contract.negators
499	    survivors: dict[str, str] = {}
500	    for clause in _clauses(span):
501	        out_idx = [i for i, _ in _match_spans(clause, outcome_vocab)]
502	        if not out_idx:
503	            continue                                         # this clause is not about our outcome
504	        neg_idx = {i for i, _ in _match_spans(clause, neg_pats)}
505	        for cls, pats in contract.polarity.items():
506	            for ti, form in _match_spans(clause, pats):
507	                if not any(abs(ti - oi) <= DIR_WINDOW for oi in out_idx):
508	                    continue                                 # cue is too far from the outcome it governs
509	                if any(0 < ti - ni <= NEG_WINDOW for ni in neg_idx):
510	                    continue                                 # negated: DISCARD, do not flip
511	                survivors.setdefault(cls, form)
512	    if len(survivors) != 1:
513	        return Facet('direction')                            # no cue, or the outcome moves both ways
514	    cls, form = next(iter(survivors.items()))
515	    return Facet('direction', cls, 'span', form)
516

518	def span_numbers(card: dict) -> list[str]:
519	    """The figures that STAND AS THEIR OWN NUMBER in the verbatim span.
520	
521	    `card['has_number']` is computed at extraction as `bool(re.search(r'\\d', claim))` -- it reads the
522	    MODEL-AUTHORED CLAIM. It is not evidence and it is not trusted here. (It also undercounts: 29 spans
523	    carry a span-verified figure and only 16 cards are flagged, because the paraphrase dropped the number.)
524	    """
525	    span, year = card.get('span') or '', str(card.get('year') or '')
526	    # a version number ("Industry 4.0") and a bare year are not effect sizes
527	    s = re.sub(r'\b[A-Z][A-Za-z&.]*\s+\d\.\d\b', ' ', span)
528	    s = re.sub(r'\b(?:1[89]|20)\d\d\b', ' ', s)
529	    return [n for n in re.findall(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?', s) if len(n) >= 2 and n != year]
530	
531	
532	def span_eligibility(span: str, contract: ResearchContract) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
533	    """IS THIS SPAN THE PAPER'S OWN, MEASURED FINDING? Two failures, two DIFFERENT severities.
534	
535	      SECOND-HAND (fatal) -- the span reports somebody ELSE'S study. The finding, and the figure, belong
536	        to a paper we have not read and are not citing. Printing it under this paper's name is a
537	        fabricated binding: "Writing in the Eurasian Business Review in 2021, Damioli et al. show that
538	        [Graetz and Michaels' result, and their number]". Such a card may not be attributed AT ALL, and
539	        may not enter a comparison. This is fraud, and it is invisible to every gate we have, because
540	        the span check asks "is this text in the paper?" and never "is this FINDING the paper's OWN?"
541	
542	      FORECAST (soft) -- the span projects rather than measures. Nothing was estimated, so there is
543	        nothing to weigh against an estimate, and a projection placed in "conflict" with a measurement
544	        is a manufactured conflict. But it remains perfectly CITABLE as what it is -- an argument, an
545	        expectation -- so it is barred from COMPARISONS ONLY. Barring it from the prose as well would
546	        starve the corpus, and a gate that eats its own evidence is a failure this codebase has already
547	        paid for once.
548	
549	    Returns (fatal, soft). Nothing is repaired; everything is recorded and counted.
550	    """
551	    fatal, soft = [], []
552	    for pat in contract.secondhand_cues:
553	        m = re.search(pat, span, re.I)
554	        if m:
555	            fatal.append(f'SECOND_HAND: the span reports another study ("{m.group(0)[:40]}") -- the '
556	                         f'finding is not this paper\'s own')
557	            break
558	    for pat in contract.forecast_cues:
559	        m = re.search(pat, span, re.I)
560	        if m:
561	            soft.append(f'FORECAST: the span projects rather than measures ("{m.group(0)[:30]}") -- '
562	                        f'citable as an argument, but there is no estimate to adjudicate')
563	            break
564	    return fatal, soft
565

567	@dataclass
568	class CardFacets:
569	    card_id: str
570	    doi: str
571	    facets: dict[str, Facet]
572	    outcomes_all: list[str]
573	    industries_all: list[str]
574	    numbers: list[str]
575	    ineligibility: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # FATAL: may not be attributed at all
576	    not_adjudicable: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # SOFT: citable, but not a comparison term
577	    #: RUNG 4 obligation 2 — for each DECLARED facet, the verbatim span excerpt that supports it (or ''
578	    #: if the span never states it). A declared value the span does not corroborate is a string off the
579	    #: card; a verdict may not turn on it.
580	    facet_spans: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
581	
582	    def f(self, name: str) -> Facet:
583	        return self.facets.get(name, Facet(name))
584	
585	    def facet_span(self, name: str) -> str:
586	        return self.facet_spans.get(name, '')
587	
588	    @property
589	    def quantitative(self) -> bool:
590	        return bool(self.numbers)
591	
592	    @property
593	    def eligible(self) -> bool:
594	        """May this card be an ATTRIBUTED clause under its OWN authors' names? (No, if second-hand.)"""
595	        return not self.ineligibility
596	
597	    @property
598	    def adjudicable(self) -> bool:
599	        """May this card be a TERM IN A COMPARISON? (No, if second-hand OR a projection.)"""
600	        return not self.ineligibility and not self.not_adjudicable

603	def derive_facets(card: dict, contract: ResearchContract) -> CardFacets:
604	    """The 8-facet key. DECLARED fields are read off the card; the rest come from the SPAN, or are MISSING."""
605	    span = card.get('span') or ''
606	    fx: dict[str, Facet] = {}
607	    facet_spans: dict[str, str] = {}
608	    for fname, field_name in contract.declared_facets.items():
609	        v = (card.get(field_name) or '').strip()
610	        fx[fname] = Facet(fname, v, 'declared') if v else Facet(fname)
611	        # RUNG 4 obligation 2: a DECLARED facet is only usable in a verdict if the SPAN corroborates it.
612	        # The unit of analysis is the axis a reconciliation turns on, so its span-binding is load-bearing;
613	        # `level_span_support` returns the verbatim excerpt, or '' when the card asserts a level the span
614	        # never states (the exact way argument_planner.py:599 used to trust a string off the card).
615	        if v and fname == 'unit_of_analysis':
616	            facet_spans[fname] = level_span_support(span, v)
617	    for fname, vocab in contract.span_facets.items():
618	        fx[fname] = derive_span_facet(span, fname, vocab)
619	    # OUTCOME AND DIRECTION ARE DERIVED TOGETHER. Derived apart, they need not be about each other --
620	    # and on the real corpus they were not. This OVERWRITES the independent outcome tag above.
621	    o_facet, d_facet, ambiguous = derive_outcome_direction(span, contract)
622	    fx[contract.outcome_facet] = o_facet
623	    fx['direction'] = d_facet
624	    _elig = span_eligibility(span, contract)
625	    return CardFacets(
626	        card_id=card['id'], doi=card.get('doi', ''), facets=fx,
627	        outcomes_all=derive_span_facet_all(span, 'outcome', contract.span_facets.get('outcome', {})),
628	        industries_all=derive_span_facet_all(span, 'industry', contract.span_facets.get('industry', {})),
629	        numbers=span_numbers(card),
630	        ineligibility=_elig[0], not_adjudicable=_elig[1] + ambiguous,
631	        facet_spans=facet_spans,
632	    )

636	
637	BUNDLE_KINDS = {
638	    'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT':  'same outcome, different unit of analysis -- the classic '
639	                                    '"they only look contradictory" case',
640	    'SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION': 'same unit, same outcome, opposite direction -- a GENUINE conflict',
641	    'SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD': 'same outcome, same direction, different identification strategy '
642	                                     '-- robustness',
643	    'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON': 'same outcome, different time horizon',
644	    'UNCOUNTERED':                  'a finding with NO counterpart anywhere in the corpus -- a boundary',
645	    'COVERAGE_GAP':                 'an empty cell of the evidence matrix -- a derivable research gap',
646	    'NOT_A_COMPARISON':             'the axis this would turn on is NOT DECLARED on both cards',
647	}
648	
649	
650	@dataclass
651	class Bundle:
652	    kind: str
653	    axis: str                       # the facet the comparison TURNS ON
654	    card_ids: list[str]
655	    shared: dict[str, str]          # facets held CONSTANT
656	    varies: dict[str, str]          # card_id -> value on the axis
657	    operation: str                  # the synthesis_contract operation this licenses
658	    apparent_conflict: bool         # do the two cards actually LOOK contradictory?
659	    comparable: bool                # are these two ESTIMATES on the same footing?
660	    incomparability: list[str]      # ...and if not, exactly why not
661	    score: float
662	    note: str = ''
663	    evidence_tier: str = ''         # estimates | findings | positions -- WHAT KIND of thing conflicts
664	
665	    def key(self) -> tuple:
666	        return (self.kind, self.axis, tuple(sorted(self.card_ids)))
667	
668	
669	def _evidence_tier(a: CardFacets, b: CardFacets, contract: ResearchContract) -> str:
670	    """WHAT KIND OF THING IS IN TENSION HERE? The verdict is only allowed to say what is true of it.
671	
672	    'The evidence conflicts' is a claim ABOUT EVIDENCE. Two theoretical models that reach opposite
673	    conclusions are not evidence in conflict -- they are FRAMEWORKS in disagreement, and a review that
674	    reports the second as the first has overstated the literature. This tier decides which sentence the
675	    planner is permitted to license, and it is the difference between an adjudication and an overclaim.
676	    """
677	    emp = set(contract.empirical_designs)
678	    ma, mb = a.f('method'), b.f('method')
679	    a_emp = ma.known and ma.value in emp
680	    b_emp = mb.known and mb.value in emp
681	    if not a_emp and not b_emp:
682	        return 'positions'                      # two models/reviews: frameworks disagree, not evidence
683	    if a_emp != b_emp:
684	        # ONE MODEL, ONE MEASUREMENT. This is neither "the frameworks disagree" nor "the estimates
685	        # disagree" -- it is a prediction that has not been reconciled with the one measurement we hold,
686	        # and saying exactly that is both true and more interesting than either alternative. Both of the
687	        # genuine conflicts this corpus actually contains are of this kind.
688	        return 'model_vs_measurement'
689	    if a.quantitative and b.quantitative:
690	        return 'estimates'
691	    return 'findings'
692	
693	
694	def _comparability(a: CardFacets, b: CardFacets, contract: ResearchContract) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
695	    """ARE THESE TWO ESTIMATES ON THE SAME FOOTING? Almost never, and the plan must SAY so.
696	
697	    Two numbers are comparable only if they are numbers, produced by designs that estimate something,
698	    about the same outcome, at the same unit, over the same horizon. Anything else and 'X is bigger than
699	    Y' is a category error. A theory paper and a survey do not disagree about a magnitude; they are not
700	    both reporting a magnitude.
701	    """
702	    why: list[str] = []
703	    emp = set(contract.empirical_designs)
704	    for c, tag in ((a, 'first'), (b, 'second')):
705	        m = c.f('method')
706	        if not m.known:
707	            why.append(f'the {tag} card declares no method')
708	        elif m.value not in emp:
709	            why.append(f'the {tag} card is a {m.value} paper -- it states a position, not an estimate')
710	        if not c.quantitative:
711	            why.append(f'the {tag} card reports no figure in its span -- there is no estimate to compare')
712	    ua, ub = a.f('unit_of_analysis'), b.f('unit_of_analysis')
713	    if ua.known and ub.known and ua.value != ub.value:
714	        why.append(f'they observe different units of analysis ({ua.value} vs {ub.value})')
715	    ha, hb = a.f('horizon'), b.f('horizon')
716	    if not (ha.known and hb.known):
717	        why.append('at least one card declares no horizon, so the time base cannot be matched')
718	    elif ha.value != hb.value:
719	        why.append(f'they observe different horizons ({ha.value} vs {hb.value})')
720	    oa, ob = a.f('outcome'), b.f('outcome')
721	    if oa.known and ob.known and oa.value != ob.value:
722	        why.append(f'they measure different outcomes ({oa.value} vs {ob.value})')
723	    return (not why), why
724	
725	
726	def _apparent_conflict(a: CardFacets, b: CardFacets) -> bool:
727	    """Do these two cards actually LOOK contradictory? You may not reconcile what was never in tension.
728	
729	    Requires BOTH directions to be span-derived and KNOWN and OPPOSED. An UNKNOWN direction is not a
730	    conflict; it is an absence of information, and treating it as a conflict is how a report ends up
731	    'reconciling' two papers that agreed all along.
732	    """
733	    da, db = a.f('direction'), b.f('direction')
734	    if not (da.known and db.known):
735	        return False
736	    return {da.value, db.value} in ({'positive', 'negative'},
737	                                    {'positive', 'null'}, {'negative', 'null'})
738	
739	
740	def _score(a: CardFacets, b: CardFacets, apparent: bool, comparable: bool) -> float:
741	    s = 0.0
742	    s += 3.0 * (a.quantitative + b.quantitative)      # a comparison of FIGURES is worth most
743	    s += 4.0 if apparent else 0.0                     # a genuine tension is the point of a review
744	    s += 2.0 if comparable else 0.0
745	    for c in (a, b):
746	        if c.f('method').known and c.f('method').value in ('experiment', 'quasi-experimental'):
747	            s += 1.5
748	        if c.f('horizon').known:
749	            s += 0.5
750	    return s
751	
752	
753	def find_bundles(cfs: list[CardFacets], contract: ResearchContract,
754	                 cards_by_id: dict[str, dict]) -> list[Bundle]:
755	    """Every comparison this corpus can actually support -- and no others."""
756	    out: list[Bundle] = []
757	    by_id = {c.card_id: c for c in cfs}
758	
759	    # A SECOND-HAND OR FORECAST SPAN IS NOT A TERM IN AN ARGUMENT. Excluded here, at the source, so no
760	    # bundle anywhere downstream can be built on one. Before this line, FOUR of the five "genuine
761	    # conflicts" this planner found were artifacts: the top-scoring one set Acemoglu's theoretical model
762	    # against a World Economic Forum press-release projection quoted inside a review paper, and called it
763	    # a conflict in the literature.
764	    eligible = [c for c in cfs if c.adjudicable]
765	
766	    def pair_ok(a: CardFacets, b: CardFacets) -> bool:
767	        # A CARD CANNOT BE COMPARED WITH ITSELF, AND A PAPER CANNOT CORROBORATE ITSELF.
768	        # synthesis_contract rejects this as `premises_share_a_single_source`; we must not hand the
769	        # writer a "comparison" that its own gate will refuse. (Damioli 2021 carries three productivity
770	        # cards at two levels -- a tempting, and entirely invalid, level contrast.)
771	        return a.doi != b.doi
772	
773	    for a, b in itertools.combinations(eligible, 2):
774	        if not pair_ok(a, b):
775	            continue
776	        oa, ob = a.f('outcome'), b.f('outcome')
777	        if not (oa.known and ob.known and oa.value == ob.value):
778	            continue                                          # no shared dependent variable: not a comparison
779	        outcome = oa.value
780	        ua, ub = a.f('unit_of_analysis'), b.f('unit_of_analysis')
781	        ha, hb = a.f('horizon'), b.f('horizon')
782	        ma, mb = a.f('method'), b.f('method')
783	        da, db = a.f('direction'), b.f('direction')
784	        apparent = _apparent_conflict(a, b)
785	        comparable, why = _comparability(a, b, contract)
786	        tier = _evidence_tier(a, b, contract)
787	        sc = _score(a, b, apparent, comparable)
788	        shared = {'outcome': outcome}
789	        for fn in ('technology', 'industry'):
790	            fa, fb = a.f(fn), b.f(fn)
791	            if fa.known and fb.known and fa.value == fb.value:
792	                shared[fn] = fa.value
793	
794	        # ---- 1. SAME OUTCOME, DIFFERENT UNIT -- the "they only look contradictory" case
795	        if ua.known and ub.known and ua.value != ub.value:
796	            out.append(Bundle(
797	                kind='SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT', axis='unit_of_analysis',
798	                card_ids=[a.card_id, b.card_id], shared=shared,
799	                varies={a.card_id: ua.value, b.card_id: ub.value},
800	                operation='CONTRASTS_LEVEL', apparent_conflict=apparent, evidence_tier=tier,
801	                comparable=comparable, incomparability=why, score=sc,
802	                note=('the estimates point in opposite directions and differ on exactly one declared '
803	                      'axis -- a reconciliation is licensed'
804	                      if apparent else
805	                      'the directions are not both known, so there is NO apparent conflict to '
806	                      'reconcile; the plan states that the evidence bears on two different units, and '
807	                      'does NOT claim the findings agree')))
808	        elif ua.value == ub.value and ua.known:
809	            # ---- 2. SAME UNIT, OPPOSITE DIRECTION -- a genuine conflict (only if BOTH directions known)
810	            if apparent:
811	                out.append(Bundle(
812	                    kind='SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION', axis='direction',
813	                    card_ids=[a.card_id, b.card_id], shared={**shared, 'unit_of_analysis': ua.value},
814	                    varies={a.card_id: da.value, b.card_id: db.value},
815	                    operation='CONTRASTS_DIRECTION', apparent_conflict=True, evidence_tier=tier,
816	                    comparable=comparable, incomparability=why, score=sc + 2,
817	                    note='same outcome, same declared unit, opposed span-derived directions -- this '
818	                         'conflict cannot be dissolved by pointing at the unit of analysis'))
819	            # ---- 3. SAME FINDING, DIFFERENT METHOD -- robustness
820	            elif (ma.known and mb.known and ma.value != mb.value
821	                  and da.known and db.known and da.value == db.value):
822	                out.append(Bundle(
823	                    kind='SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD', axis='method',
824	                    card_ids=[a.card_id, b.card_id],
825	                    shared={**shared, 'unit_of_analysis': ua.value, 'direction': da.value},
826	                    varies={a.card_id: ma.value, b.card_id: mb.value},
827	                    operation='RANK_EVIDENCE', apparent_conflict=False, evidence_tier=tier,
828	                    comparable=comparable, incomparability=why, score=sc + 1,
829	                    note='the same directional finding survives a change of identification strategy'))
830	
831	        # ---- 4. SAME OUTCOME, DIFFERENT HORIZON (only when BOTH declare one -- 43% do not)
832	        if ha.known and hb.known and ha.value != hb.value:
833	            out.append(Bundle(
834	                kind='SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON', axis='horizon',
835	                card_ids=[a.card_id, b.card_id], shared=shared,
836	                varies={a.card_id: ha.value, b.card_id: hb.value},
837	                operation='CONTRASTS_HORIZON', apparent_conflict=apparent, evidence_tier=tier,
838	                comparable=comparable, incomparability=why, score=sc,
839	                note='' if apparent else 'no apparent conflict: this is a horizon SPREAD, not a horizon '
840	                                         'DISPUTE'))
841	
842	    # ---- 5. THE REFUSALS. Two cards on the same outcome whose axis is NOT DECLARED are NOT a comparison.
843	    #         We emit them so the refusal is VISIBLE and auditable, instead of silently inventing a tag.
844	    for a, b in itertools.combinations(eligible, 2):
845	        if not pair_ok(a, b):
846	            continue
847	        oa, ob = a.f('outcome'), b.f('outcome')
848	        if not (oa.known and ob.known and oa.value == ob.value):
849	            continue
850	        missing = [fn for fn in ('unit_of_analysis', 'horizon', 'method')
851	                   if not (a.f(fn).known and b.f(fn).known)]
852	        if missing and not any(bd.card_ids == [a.card_id, b.card_id] for bd in out):
853	            out.append(Bundle(
854	                kind='NOT_A_COMPARISON', axis=','.join(missing),
855	                card_ids=[a.card_id, b.card_id], shared={'outcome': oa.value}, varies={},
856	                operation='', apparent_conflict=False, comparable=False,
857	                incomparability=[f'{fn} is not declared on both cards' for fn in missing],
858	                score=0.0,
859	                note='REFUSED: the axis this would turn on is missing. Inventing it here is exactly how '
860	                     'a false reconciliation is manufactured, so no comparison is planned.'))
861	
862	    # ---- 6. UNCOUNTERED: a cell of (outcome x unit) that exactly ONE source occupies -> a boundary
863	    cell: dict[tuple[str, str], set[str]] = {}
864	    for c in eligible:
865	        o, u = c.f('outcome'), c.f('unit_of_analysis')
866	        if o.known and u.known:
867	            cell.setdefault((o.value, u.value), set()).add(c.doi)
868	    for c in eligible:
869	        o, u = c.f('outcome'), c.f('unit_of_analysis')
870	        if not (o.known and u.known):
871	            continue
872	        if len(cell[(o.value, u.value)]) == 1 and c.quantitative:
873	            out.append(Bundle(
874	                kind='UNCOUNTERED', axis='unit_of_analysis', card_ids=[c.card_id],
875	                shared={'outcome': o.value, 'unit_of_analysis': u.value}, varies={},
876	                operation='COVERAGE_GAP', apparent_conflict=False, comparable=False,
877	                evidence_tier=('estimates' if c.quantitative else 'findings'),
878	                incomparability=['there is no second source in this cell to compare against'],
879	                score=2.0 + len(c.numbers),
880	                note='a span-verified figure that NO other source in the corpus counters at the same '
881	                     'unit -- the review can state its scope, and must not generalise beyond it'))
882	
883	    dedup: dict[tuple, Bundle] = {}
884	    for b in out:
885	        k = b.key()
886	        if k not in dedup or b.score > dedup[k].score:
887	            dedup[k] = b
888	    return sorted(dedup.values(), key=lambda b: -b.score)
889	
890	
891	def coverage_gaps(cfs: list[CardFacets], contract: ResearchContract) -> list[dict]:
892	    """THE OUTLINE WRITES CHEQUES THE CORPUS CANNOT CASH. Here is the bank statement.
893	
894	    An empty cell is a DERIVABLE research gap (synthesis_contract calls this COVERAGE_GAP and it is the
895	    one operation that needs no second premise). It is also the honest reason to DELETE a subsection:
896	    a promised 4-subsection industry section over a corpus with zero retail cards produces prose that
897	    lists nothing, and two criteria regressed for exactly that.
898	    """
899	    outs = list(contract.span_facets.get('outcome', {}))
900	    inds = list(contract.span_facets.get('industry', {}))
901	    matrix: dict[tuple[str, str], set[str]] = {}
902	    for c in cfs:
903	        for o in c.outcomes_all:
904	            for i in c.industries_all:
905	                matrix.setdefault((o, i), set()).add(c.doi)
906	    gaps = []
907	    for i in inds:
908	        srcs = {d for (o, ii), ds in matrix.items() if ii == i for d in ds}
909	        gaps.append({'industry': i, 'sources': len(srcs),
910	                     'outcomes_covered': sorted({o for (o, ii) in matrix if ii == i})})
911	    return sorted(gaps, key=lambda g: g['sources'])

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE COHESION PASS — Sol plan 3, item 7. It targets S2 Paragraph Cohesion = 4.90, OUR LOWEST SCORE.
3	
4	The judge's words: "fragmented narrative... without adequate transitions". They are correct, and the
5	cause is structural, not stylistic: 26 subsections were written by 26 INDEPENDENT LLM calls, each of
6	which could see its own cards and NOTHING ELSE. No writer knew what the paragraph above it had said, so
7	no writer could move from it. A document assembled from 26 monologues reads like 26 monologues.
8	
9	** WHAT THIS PASS MAY NOT DO, AND WHY THAT IS THE ENTIRE DESIGN **
10	
11	It may not rewrite the report. Sol: "a free-form sequential rewrite is NOT safe to stack; THE
12	IMMUTABILITY OF ATTRIBUTED OBJECTS IS THE SAFETY BOUNDARY." A model handed a validated report and asked
13	to "improve the flow" will smooth an attributed clause — move a number, merge two findings, soften a
14	hedge — and every such edit is a fabrication that has already passed the gate. The gate runs BEFORE
15	this pass. There is no second gate that re-derives an edited clause against its span, because by then
16	the sentence has a receipt in the sidecar and the receipt is a hash of the text as it was.
17	
18	So the boundary is not a rule this file promises to follow. It is a PROPERTY OF THE TYPES:
19	
20	    * `Attributed` is a frozen dataclass and THIS FILE NEVER CONSTRUCTS ONE. Grep it: there is no
21	      `Attributed(` in this module. It cannot edit a clause because it cannot make one, and it cannot
22	      mutate one because the type is frozen. The attributed nodes that come in are the same OBJECTS
23	      that go out — checked by identity, not by equality, in `_assert_frozen()`.
24	    * Everything it emits is `Owned(premise_ids=())` — a FRAME sentence, the one lane in the AST that
25	      is licensed by nothing and may therefore assert nothing particular: no number, no source, no new
26	      fact. Every sentence this pass creates is then run back through `validate_report()`, the SAME
27	      gate that judges the model's sentences, and anything that does not survive is DROPPED, never
28	      repaired.
29	
30	THE FIVE PERMITTED OPERATIONS (and nothing else):
31	    1. ADD an OWNED transition that expresses ANALYTICAL MOVEMENT between two paragraphs.
32	    2. ADD an OWNED topic sentence where a paragraph opens cold on a finding.
33	    3. REORDER already-admitted paragraph objects WITHIN their subsection.
34	    4. DELETE redundant OWNED sentences (the boundary refrain, the duplicated verdict).
35	    5. Repair grammar in an OWNED sentence — never in a factual clause.
36	
37	** MOVEMENT, NOT SIGNPOSTING. ** A transition that says "Turning now to..." is not cohesion, it is a
38	table of contents read aloud, and a judge scores it as filler. The transitions here are computed from
39	the DECLARED FIELDS of the cards the two paragraphs actually cite — the analytical distance really
40	travelled: a change of LEVEL (occupation -> firm), of METHOD (RCT -> panel), of HORIZON, of SECTOR.
41	Where two paragraphs move nowhere, NOTHING IS EMITTED: silence is better than filler, and a refrain of
42	identical connective sentences is the failure mode this pass is most likely to introduce.
43	
44	    python scripts/cohesion_pass.py            # self-test, offline, no spend
45	"""
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import collections
49	import re
50	import sys
51	from pathlib import Path
52	
53	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
54	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
55	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
56	
57	from report_ast import (Attributed, Owned, Heading, ParagraphBreak,      # noqa: E402
58	                        EvidenceTable, CardBundle, validate_report)
59	
60	CONTENT = (Attributed, Owned)
61	
62	# A facet must be a WORD, not a measurement. Anything with a digit in it is a particular, and a
63	# particular in an OWNED sentence is sourced to nothing.
64	_DIGIT = re.compile(r'\d')
65	
66	# Facets, in the order a reader feels the move. Level first: "these are occupations, those are firms"
67	# is the single most load-bearing distinction in an evidence review, and it is the one the judge said
68	# our report never drew.
69	FACETS = ('level', 'method', 'horizon', 'sector')
70	_CARD_FIELD = {'level': ('level', 'unit_of_analysis'), 'method': ('method', 'design'),
71	               'horizon': ('horizon',), 'sector': ('industry',)}
72	
73	
74	def _clean_facet(v: str) -> str:
75	    v = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', str(v or '')).strip().lower().rstrip('.')
76	    if not v or _DIGIT.search(v) or len(v) > 38:
77	        return ''
78	    return v
79	
80	
81	# ===================================================================== paragraphs
82	class Para:
83	    """A paragraph, AS AN OBJECT — so that 'reorder the paragraphs' is a list operation on typed things
84	    and not a regex over prose. `nodes` holds the ORIGINAL node objects, by identity."""
85	
86	    __slots__ = ('nodes', 'sub', 'idx')
87	
88	    def __init__(self, nodes, sub='', idx=0):
89	        self.nodes = list(nodes)
90	        self.sub = sub
91	        self.idx = idx
92	
93	    @property
94	    def attributed(self):
95	        return [n for n in self.nodes if isinstance(n, Attributed)]
96	
97	    @property
98	    def has_synthesis(self) -> bool:
99	        """Does this paragraph carry an OWNED node licensed by premises? Those are the planner's
100	        deterministic VERDICTS. A verdict adjudicates the findings STATED ABOVE IT, so a paragraph
101	        holding one is PINNED: reorder it above its own evidence and it adjudicates thin air."""
102	        return any(isinstance(n, Owned) and n.premise_ids for n in self.nodes)
103	
104	    def facets(self, b: CardBundle) -> dict:
105	        out: dict = collections.defaultdict(collections.Counter)
106	        for n in self.attributed:
107	            for cl in n.clauses:
108	                r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
109	                if not r.ok:
110	                    continue
111	                for f in FACETS:
112	                    for key in _CARD_FIELD[f]:
113	                        v = _clean_facet(r.card.get(key))
114	                        if v:
115	                            out[f][v] += 1
116	                            break
117	        return {f: c for f, c in out.items() if c}
118	
119	    def dominant(self, b: CardBundle) -> dict:
120	        return {f: c.most_common(1)[0][0] for f, c in self.facets(b).items()}
121	
122	
123	def paragraphs_of(nodes: list) -> list:
124	    """Split a node list into (heading-run, [Para]) blocks. Headings, tables and breaks are STRUCTURE;
125	    only Attributed/Owned are paragraph content."""
126	    blocks: list = []                 # list of ('nodes', [...]) or ('paras', sub, [Para])
127	    cur_struct: list = []
128	    cur_para: list = []
129	    paras: list = []
130	    sub = ''
131	
132	    def flush_para():
133	        nonlocal cur_para
134	        if cur_para:
135	            paras.append(Para(cur_para, sub, len(paras)))
136	            cur_para = []
137	
138	    def flush_block():
139	        nonlocal paras
140	        flush_para()
141	        if paras:
142	            blocks.append(('paras', sub, paras))
143	            paras = []
144	
145	    for n in nodes:
146	        if isinstance(n, (Heading, EvidenceTable)):
147	            flush_block()
148	            if cur_struct:
149	                blocks.append(('nodes', cur_struct))
150	                cur_struct = []
151	            blocks.append(('nodes', [n]))
152	            if isinstance(n, Heading) and n.level >= 3:
153	                sub = n.text
154	            elif isinstance(n, Heading):
155	                sub = n.text
156	            continue
157	        if isinstance(n, ParagraphBreak):
158	            flush_para()
159	            continue
160	        if isinstance(n, CONTENT):
161	            cur_para.append(n)
162	    flush_block()
163	    if cur_struct:
164	        blocks.append(('nodes', cur_struct))
165	    return blocks
166	
167	
168	# ===================================================================== movement
169	def movement(prev: Para, cur: Para, b: CardBundle) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
170	    """The analytical distance ACTUALLY travelled between two paragraphs. -> (facet, from, to) or ()."""
171	    a, c = prev.dominant(b), cur.dominant(b)
172	    for f in FACETS:
173	        if f in a and f in c and a[f] != c[f]:
174	            return (f, a[f], c[f])
175	    return ()
176	
177	
178	# The transition is a claim about WHAT KIND OF EVIDENCE is being set against what kind — never a
179	# signpost. Two phrasings per facet, chosen by a hash of the paragraph, so that a document with nine
180	# level-moves does not contain the same sentence nine times. (A refrain reads worse than a gap.)
181	TEMPLATES: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
182	    'level': (
183	        'The evidence to this point is measured at the {a} level; what follows is measured at the {b} '
184	        'level, and the two do not answer the same question.',
185	        'Moving from the {a} level to the {b} level changes what the evidence is able to show, and the '
186	        'shift is not merely one of scale.',
187	    ),

224	def _assert_frozen(before: list, after: list) -> None:
225	    """THE SAFETY BOUNDARY, CHECKED BY IDENTITY.
226	
227	    Not `==`: a model-rewritten clause that happened to compare equal would pass an equality check, and
228	    an `Attributed` rebuilt from edited text with the same card_id compares UNEQUAL only if the text
229	    changed — which is what we want, but it would still be a NEW OBJECT. Checking `id()` proves the
230	    stronger thing: every attributed node on the way out IS the very object that came in. Nothing was
231	    edited, nothing was rebuilt, nothing was added, nothing was dropped.
232	    """
233	    xs = [n for n in before if isinstance(n, Attributed)]
234	    ys = [n for n in after if isinstance(n, Attributed)]
235	    if len(xs) != len(ys):
236	        raise AssertionError(f'COHESION PASS ADDED OR DROPPED AN ATTRIBUTED NODE: {len(xs)} -> {len(ys)}')
237	    if collections.Counter(id(n) for n in xs) != collections.Counter(id(n) for n in ys):
238	        raise AssertionError('COHESION PASS REPLACED AN ATTRIBUTED OBJECT — the clauses are not frozen')

242	    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9 ]', '', re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (s or '').lower())).strip()
